name: Chicken Fried Steak and Cream Gravy
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the steak and pound it to 1/2 inch thickness if needed; cut into 6 to 8 pieces.
2. In a bowl, combine evaporated milk, Tabasco sauce, and salt.
3. In another bowl, measure 1 cup of flour.
4. In a separate bowl, combine the remaining flour, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
5. Dip each steak piece into the flour mixture, then into the milk mixture, and finally coat it again with the seasoned flour mixture.
6. Set aside until all meat is coated.
7. Heat 1 to 2 inches of oil in a frying pan.
8. Fry the steaks until both sides are golden brown, about 2 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels.
9. Serve with cream gravy, mashed potatoes, and gravy.
CREAM SAUCE:
Pour off all but 6 tablespoons of fat from the frying pan; add 6 tablespoons flour into the pan (using any leftover seasoned flour) and blend well. Gradually stir in 2 1/2 cups milk. Cook and stir over medium heat until thickened. Season with salt and pepper. Cover steaks with gravy when served. Makes 2 cups.
